# sim.admixture_demography { #tspaint.sim.admixture_demography }

```python
tspaint.sim.admixture_demography(
    Ne=10000,
    T_admix=30.0,
    census_offset=1.0,
    T_split=2000.0,
    f_A=0.3,
    migration_rate=0.0,
)
```

Build a two-source admixture demography with a post-pulse census.

Two sources (A, B) feed an admixed population, with a census placed just
older than the admixture pulse. A and B contribute fractions ``f_A`` and
``1 - f_A`` to ADMIX at ``T_admix``; they merge into a common ancestor at
``T_split``. The census sits at ``T_admix + census_offset`` (strictly
between admixture and split).

Two demographic regimes (CLAUDE.md §9 — the harder, more realistic one is
the migration model, where the sources are less differentiated):

* ``migration_rate == 0`` — the sources are **isolated** between split and
  admixture (the clean two-source pulse).
* ``migration_rate > 0`` — symmetric **low-level gene flow** between A and B
  over ``[census_time, T_split)`` (the interval where they coexist as
  distinct populations after the census). The census still cleanly labels
  each lineage's source — migration only acts *deeper* than the census — so
  the local-ancestry truth is unchanged, but A and B share more recent
  ancestry, making them harder to tell apart.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**Ne**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10000]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Diploid effective population size for every population.

<code>[**T_admix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [30.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Time (generations ago) of the admixture pulse forming ADMIX.

<code>[**census_offset**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Offset added to ``T_admix`` to place the census (must keep it strictly between admixture and split).

<code>[**T_split**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Time (generations ago) at which A and B coalesce into ANCESTRAL.

<code>[**f_A**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.3]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Fraction of ADMIX contributed by source A (B contributes ``1 - f_A``).

<code>[**migration_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Per-generation symmetric migration rate between A and B over ``[census_time, T_split)``. ``0`` (default) gives the isolated model.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[msprime](`msprime`).[Demography](`msprime.Demography`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Demography with populations A, B, ADMIX and ANCESTRAL plus the admixture, census and split events (and the A↔B migration if requested).

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

<code>[:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ValueError](`ValueError`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   If ``T_admix < T_admix + census_offset < T_split`` is violated, or ``migration_rate < 0``.
